mission.title: external-secrets: Creating External Config Maps
mission.description: Creating External Config Maps. This issue affects 46+ users.

steps.title: Identify external-secrets feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your external-secrets deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=external-secrets
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Creating External Config Maps

steps.title: Check current Configmap configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets resources:
```bash
kubectl get configmap -A
kubectl describe configmap <name> -n <namespace>
```
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

steps.title: Apply the fix for Creating External Config Maps
steps.description:
## Problem Statement

What is the problem you're trying to solve?

## Related Issue

Proposal for  #2503 

## Proposed Changes

This is a design document for change proposal on `spec.target` behavior. Allowing it to target multiple manifests, and a change on `spec.target.template.target` behavior, a
```yaml
generate:
  synchronize: true
  generateExisting: true
  apiVersion: v1
  kind: ConfigMap
  name: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
  namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}"
  data:
    data: "{{ request.object.data | items(@, 'key', 'value') | object_from_lists([].key, map(&base64_decode(@), [].value)) }}"
```

steps.title: Confirm Creating External Config Maps is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: ## Problem Statement

What is the problem you're trying to solve?

## Related Issue

Proposal for  #2503 

## Proposed Changes

This is a design document for change proposal on `spec.target` behavior. Allowing it to target multiple manifests, and a change on `spec.target.template.target` behavior, also allowing it to target multiple keys.

## Checklist

- [X] All commits are signed with `git commi. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

  data:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
